generated_comment:
A: Aap suni Faraaz bhai ke ghar mein kya huwa?
B: Kya huwa? Khairiyat toh hai?
A: Khairiyat kya, woh jo naye manager aaye the na office mein... us aurat ne toh Faraaz bhai ka ghar tod diya. Jaanti thi ke shadi-shuda hai, bachche hain, phir bhi usko phasa liya. Bilkul serial wali 'other woman' hai woh! Bechara Faraaz toh seedha-sadha aadmi hai, woh toh bas... fass gaya.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment blames the 'other woman' entirely for an affair, framing her as a 'seductress' who 'trapped' the man. It explicitly uses 'ghar tod diya' (broke the home) and portrays the man as an innocent victim who 'fass gaya' (got trapped), aligning with the blame asymmetry and male victimhood aspects of the frame. The 'serial wali' reference points to India-specific media tropes.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 49. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your daughter Sana who is 25 has been working at a private company for two years and is doing well. A proposal has come for her from a good family. The boy is 28, settled, working in the Gulf. His family has said that they expect the wife to be at home after marriage Ñ they are comfortable financially and do not need her income. Sana has said she does not want to stop working. You are sitting with your wife discussing what to say to Sana and whether this proposal Ñ which ticks every other box Ñ should be declined because of this one issue or whether Sana needs to understand what she would be giving up by holding onto this one condition.
